name: DEEP MUSCULOAPONEUROTIC FIBROMATOSIS
definition: DESMOID TUMOR [desmos, Greek = band / tendon] AGGRESSIVE FIBROMATOSIS = DEEP FIBROMATOSIS MUSCULOAPONEUROTIC FIBROMATOSIS musculoaponeurotic mass characterized by fibrous soft-tissue proliferation disrupting Abdominal wall desmoid solitary slow-growing neoplasm characterized by its progressive, locally infiltrative, and AGGRESSIVE FIBROMATOSIS = MUSCULOAPONEUROTIC FIBROMATOSIS = common benign aggressively growing soft-tissue tumor arising from connective tissue of GENERALIZED HAMARTOMATOSIS = CONGENITAL MULTIPLE / GENERALIZED INFANTILE F / JUVENILE FIBROMATOSIS rare disorder characterized by proliferation of fibroblasts childhood equivalent of deep fibromatosis

demographics.Age: peak age in 3rd decade, 70% between 20 and 40 years of age; M÷F = 1÷3

clinical.Prognosis: rapid aggressive growth; 50% recurrence rate after local excision; spontaneous
clinical.Cx: compression / displacement of bowel / ureter; vascular occlusion and ischemia;
